note: Slick Extras is an add-on/scaffolding for the Slick carousel module. Its only substantive runtime contribution is a SlickSkin plugin adding six extra CSS skins; the rest is example (slick_example) and development (slick_devel) sub-modules. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded), probing documented facts an agent would otherwise read source for; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site, which module must be installed for Slick Extras to work, and what does Slick Extras add on top of it?
evals.expected_output: Slick Extras depends on the Slick module (slick:slick >= 3.x) and adds extra, less-common CSS skins (via a SlickSkin plugin) plus example/development sub-modules.

evals.prompt: How does Slick Extras register its extra carousel skins on a Drupal site? Name the plugin type and the class.
evals.expected_output: It implements Slick's SlickSkin plugin type with class SlickExtrasSkin (plugin id slick_extras_skin) extending SlickSkinPluginBase, overriding setSkins().

evals.prompt: Name some of the extra skins Slick Extras adds to the Slick optionset Skin dropdown.
evals.expected_output: Six skins: d3-back (X 3d back), boxed (X Boxed), boxed-carousel (X Box carousel), boxed-split (X Box split), rounded (X Rounded), and vtabs (X VTabs, a thumbnail skin).

evals.prompt: I enabled Slick Extras but its skins don't appear in the Slick optionset Skin dropdown. What should I do?
evals.expected_output: Skins are cached; clear the cache (e.g. drush cr) and they will appear in the Skin dropdown.

evals.prompt: I want to register my own Slick skin the way Slick Extras does. What's the pattern?
evals.expected_output: Create src/Plugin/slick/YourSkin.php with a @SlickSkin annotation, extend SlickSkinPluginBase, override setSkins() to return skin definitions with a css theme file, ship the CSS, and clear cache.

evals.prompt: Which Slick Extras sub-module provides sample optionsets (prefixed X), image styles, and a demo View, and what extra module does it need?
evals.expected_output: The Slick Example (slick_example) sub-module ships sample X-prefixed optionsets, Slick image styles, and the slick_x View; it depends on Slick Views (slick_views).

evals.prompt: Is Slick Extras intended for direct use in production, or is it example/scaffolding code?
evals.expected_output: It is example/scaffolding: the maintainers recommend cloning what you need and uninstalling it in production (though it is safe to leave enabled). The base module offers extra skins; the example/devel sub-modules are for learning.

evals.prompt: What does the Slick Development (slick_devel) sub-module of Slick Extras provide?
evals.expected_output: slick_devel provides a settings form (route slick_devel.settings) and a debug JS loader used while developing the Slick library.
